data.title: Surf Air Mobility: After The Drop, Is The AI And Electrification Story Attractive?
data.description:
Surf Air Mobility Inc. (SRFM) stock has fallen nearly 35% since my last report. I consider the stock a very speculative play on AI and the electrification of aviation. The company is backed by Palantir (PLTR), and in my prior report, I also noted that a bullish stance on Surf Air Mobility because it is backed by Palantir Technologies was not justified. In this report, I discuss the company’s Q3 2025 earnings and update my stock price target.

Surf Air Mobility Leans On Commercial Opportunities In AI And Electrification

The appeal of Surf Air Mobility is definitely not their charter business. It is, however, the only part of the business that is currently profitable on an adjusted EBITDA basis. The bigger opportunities for Surf Air Mobility are technology and electrification solutions.

The technology solution is SurfOS, which consists of three components:

  1. BrokerOS for matching operations with clients for charter flights.
  2. OperatorOS to improve operational efficiency for flight equipment and crews.
  3. OwnerOS for advanced insights for private aircraft owners.

The platform is currently not generating revenues. The first revenues are expected to come in H1 2026. After the third quarter ended, the company raised $26 million in fresh capital for the commercialization of SurfOS and $74 million in convertible notes. This also highlights the dilution risks as the charter operations are not sustaining the development of technology and electrification solutions.

The third opportunity for Surf Air Mobility is the electrification of Cessna Grand Caravan EX airplanes. Certification has previously been delayed to 2027, so until then, we see no revenues from this effort and development, and certification is likely going to be costly with a risk of additional delays and levels of cash drains that may require additional funding if software and charter cash inflows are not sufficient.

Surf Air Mobility Sees EBITDA Loss Widen

Sequentially, sales rose $1.8 million to $29.2 million from charter operations. The EBITDA loss widened from $9.5 million to $9.9 million, which provides somewhat of an indication that charter profitability is not pacing with the negative cash and EBITDA generated in the other parts of the business. For the full year, the revenues are expected to be $105 million with profitable charter operations, but given the cash drains for SurfOS and electric power train development for the Cessna Grand Caravan, the company will remain loss making.

Surf Air Mobility Investment Case Leans On Electrification Of Airplanes

Absent of profits, I am using an EV/Sales valuation method to determine the stock price target based on sales estimates available on Seeking Alpha. I have run the valuation using two scenarios. In the first scenario, we use the company’s median EV/Sales, and in the second scenario, we use the transportation sector median. A case could be made that if substantial results are derived from the electric powertrain solutions, an aerospace multiple of 4.2x would be more appropriate. However, for the time being, I am taking a more conservative approach.

Based on a better capitalization, implied price targets for this year have risen to $2.53-$2.95, implying 8% downside in Scenario 1 and 8% upside in Scenario 2. For next year, the downside would be 11% in Scenario 1 and 3% upside in Scenario 2. In the year 2027, the upside would be 17%-35% and by 2028, this could be 43%-53% even with the convertible notes being converted into equity.

I don’t really have a price target for Surf Air Mobility as the real value additions are likely to be rendered post-2028, but we do note that, based on current capital raise expectations, where cash burns and debt maturities are to be carried through capital raises, the target for 2028 has come down by $1.06-$1.44. I believe that the current prices offer a better entry point as the stock is now closer valued to the fair value, and ideally another 10% drop in the stock price would driven the share price to an even more compelling entry point. Over time, I do believe that there is fundamental support for $3.20 first and $3.91 later. We continue to mark this a speculative opportunity as there is substantial risk on development timelines and capital requirements.

Conclusion: Surf Air Mobility Now Offers A Better Entry Point

Surf Air Mobility is not the stock that you should own if you are looking for near-term value. The real value is in the product launches next year and the commercial entry of the power train solutions. With negative cash flows in the years ahead, Surf Air Mobility remains a very speculative high-risk/high-reward play.

data.title: Trading With The KOLD And BOIL ETFs: Cautiously Bullish On Natural Gas
data.description:
Natural gas is about to enter the peak demand season in the United States, where inventories decline during winter. Each year, the injection season starts in March and runs through November. The withdrawal season runs from November through March. Prices tend to rise to annual highs during winter. With December and January NYMEX natural gas prices (NG1:COM) trading above $4.50 per MMBtu, prices are considerably higher than in November 2024, when the continuous U.S. natural gas futures price traded in a range of $2.514 to $3.563 per MMBtu.

Natural gas futures are highly volatile, and the price variance creates trading opportunities. Natural gas futures are leveraged, as they require a margin of around 10% to control one NYMEX natural gas futures contract. While the United States Natural Gas Fund, LP ETF (UNG) is unleveraged, the ProShares Ultra Bloomberg Natural Gas ETF (BOIL) and the ProShares UltraShort Bloomberg Natural Gas ETF (KOLD) are bullish and bearish products that magnify the price action in the volatile energy commodity.

NYMEX natural gas prices for December and January delivery are above $4.50

The forward curve for the CME’s NYMEX division U.S. natural gas futures is that the following levels in November 2025:

As the chart highlights, NYMEX natural gas futures for December 2025 were above $4.60 per MMBtu, while the January contract was trading at nearly $4.85 per MMBtu as of November 13, 2025.

Since mid-August 2025, the continuous NYMEX natural gas futures contract has made higher lows and higher highs.

As the chart highlights, nearby U.S. natural gas futures have rallied 77.4% from the August 25, 2025, low of $2.738 to the November 13, 2025, high of $4.856 per MMBtu. In October and November 2024, the energy commodity futures traded in $2.20 to $3.019 and $2.514 to $3.563 ranges, respectively. In November 2025, natural gas is already far higher than the top end of the 2024 October and November trading ranges.

The bullish case for U.S. natural gas

The bullish case for U.S. natural gas prices over the coming weeks and months is as follows:

  • The 2025 injection season, during which inventories increase, will conclude in November. Prices tend to rise in the early withdrawal season as stockpiles decline.
  • The uncertainty of the weather conditions and winter temperatures tends to cause natural gas prices to rise to annual highs during November, December, and January.
  • Increasing shipments of U.S. liquefied natural gas to Western Europe and other regions where prices are higher puts upward pressure on prices and downward pressure on supplies.
  • Sanctions on Russia due to the ongoing war in Ukraine could increase European demand for LNG from the United States.
  • The trend since August has been upward, with current prices exceeding those in October and November 2024. The trend is always a trader’s or investor’s best friend.

Natural gas is entering the peak demand season, which will run from November 2025 through March 2026. The winter tends to be bullish for the energy commodity.

The bearish case for U.S. natural gas

The bearish case for U.S. natural gas prices over the coming weeks and months is as follows:

As of the week ending October 31, 2025, natural gas stockpiles across the United States, at 3.915 trillion cubic feet, were 0.2% lower than at the same time in 2024 but 4.3% higher than the five-year average for late October. Higher stock levels tend to weigh on prices.

The shift in U.S. energy policy under the Trump administration supports higher U.S. production.

The long-term trend, which began with the high of August 2022 at around $10 per MMBtu, remains bearish.

As the chart illustrates, while the medium-term trend since the February 2024 low of $1.60 per MMBtu is higher, the long-term trend since the August 2022 high remains bearish. A rally above the March 2025 high of $4.908 per MMBtu would end the bearish trend, and nearby natural gas futures rose to near that price on November 13.

As the energy commodity heads into its peak demand season, we can expect significant volatility. Natural gas is the most volatile energy commodity, and the coming months could see significant price fluctuations, given the mix of bullish and bearish factors facing the market.

The most direct route for long or short risk positions in U.S. natural gas is through the liquid and volatile futures contracts traded on the CME’s NYMEX division. Each futures contract is for 10,000 MMBtus. The contract value is $46,300 at $4.63 per MMBtu. The CME’s current original margin requirement is $4,059 per contract, or 8.77% of the contract value. If a long or short position’s equity falls below $3,690 per contract, the CME’s NYMEX division requires maintenance margin payments.

The U.S. Natural Gas Fund is a liquid, unleveraged ETF that tracks U.S. natural gas futures prices. At $14.96 per share, UNG had over $703.93 million in assets under management. UNG trades an average of over 13.9 million shares daily and charges a 1.11% management fee.

Seeking Alpha’s ETF Grades for UNG are failing in the expenses and risk categories, but the highly liquid UNG receives the highest A+ grade in liquidity. The recent rally has caused the momentum grade to move to the B- level.

The most recent continuous contract NYMEX natural gas rally took the price 77.4% higher from the August 25, 2025, low of $2.738 to the November 13, 2025, high of $4.856 per MMBtu.

Over the same period, UNG moved 28.1% higher, from $11.71 to $15.00 per share, significantly underperforming the continuous futures contract.

BOIL is the bullish leveraged U.S. natural gas ETF

The Bloomberg Ultra Natural Gas 2X ETF provides double upside leverage for the already volatile U.S. natural gas price. BOIL had over $689.2 million in assets under management at $37.13 per share. BOIL trades an average of over 7.85 million shares daily and charges a 0.95% management fee.

BOIL receives similar Seeking Alpha ETF Grades as UNG, which are failing in the expense and risk categories. The highly liquid BOIL ETF receives the highest A+ grade in liquidity. While natural gas prices have rallied, the momentum grade is D-.

The daily chart shows that BOIL moved 42.9% higher from the August 26 low of $26.01 to the November 13 high of $37.16 per share. While BOIL outperformed UNG, it underperformed the continuous NYMEX natural gas futures contract.

KOLD is the bearish leveraged U.S. natural gas ETF

The Bloomberg Ultrashort Natural Gas -2X ETF provides double downside leverage for the already volatile U.S. natural gas price. KOLD had over $93.75 million in assets under management at $25.73 per share. KOLD trades an average of over 5.6 million shares daily and charges a 0.95% management fee.

KOLD receives the same Seeking Alpha ETF Grades as UNG and BOIL, which are failing in the expenses risk categories, but the liquid KOLD ETF receives a slightly lower A grade in liquidity. The recent rally caused KOLD to receive a failing grade in momentum.

The daily chart shows that KOLD moved 37.5% lower from the August 26 low of $41.10 to the November 13 low of $25.68 per share. KOLD moves higher as natural gas prices decline and vice versa.

BOIL and KOLD are leveraged products that employ options and swaps to create their bullish and bearish gearing. They are only suitable for short-term long or short-term short natural gas risk positions.

When approaching leveraged natural gas exposure with BOIL or KOLD, time and price stops are appropriate, as the gearing can cause oversized losses compared to the already volatile natural gas futures and unleveraged ETFs. Moreover, stable levels in natural gas prices will likely cause BOIL and KOLD to decline by a greater percentage, as the time decay erodes their value. The swaps and options that create leverage dramatically increase risks. The SEC website highlights the significant risks of leveraged ETF products, such as BOIL and KOLD.

The natural gas market is transitioning into the season when prices often rise as demand for the energy commodity increases during winter. I am cautiously bullish on the prospects for natural gas prices over the coming weeks but expect significant volatility. BOIL and KOLD are only appropriate as short-term trading tools, requiring careful attention to risk-reward dynamics. However, they enable market participants to capitalize on the energy commodity’s numerous trading opportunities during the early stages of the peak demand season, which begins this month.

I rate KOLD a sell and BOIL a buy due to seasonality as of November 13; however, these ratings are subject to change on an intraday basis, as natural gas remains one of the most volatile commodities in the asset class.

data.title: Jet2's 22% Sell-Off Makes No Sense: This Airline Is Now Deeply Undervalued
data.description:
Jet2 plc (OTCPK:DRTGF) stock has declined 22.6% since my last report. I have a "Buy" rating on the airline and holiday package provider, so the current stock price development is disappointing. The stock has lost altitude following a profit warning. In this report, I am reviewing the profit warning and I assess the company’s stock price target to determine whether the stock still provides an attractive opportunity.

A Look At The Profit Warning

On the Sept. 4, Jet2 shared that it had executed the summer period with 18.5 million seats. The company shared that it had flown 2% more holiday passengers and 17% more flight-only passengers. The company sees this as a token of its flexible operating model. However, ideally, growth is skewed more towards flying holiday customers. The company had guided for £449 - £496 in EBIT for FY26. However, heading into the winter, the company saw hesitance in the market with bookings occurring later, and as a result, the company reduced seats on sale from 5.8 million to 5.6 million and expects EBIT to be at the lower end of the range.

With last year’s EBIT of £446 million, this could indicate that the company no longer expects year-on-year growth in earnings. It actually shows that, as flexible as the company thinks the operating model is, the low holiday passenger growth during the summer has been an indicator of hesitance in the market, and that is affecting the company in the winter with more bookings closer to the time of travel. This makes revenue harder to predict and may cause airfares to be discounted to fill airplanes.

The company did not state any reasons for the pressure, but it is likely that the high cost of living and flight disruptions throughout Europe play a role. I believe that Jet2 is positioned well with its holiday package products, as high economic growth makes holiday packages affordable for an even wider travel audience, and lower economic growth could attract customers that would normally opt for more premium travel experiences. However, that does not mean that the company is isolated from any pressures.

Jet2 Stock Was Cheap And Even Cheaper Now

To determine multi-year price targets, The Aerospace Forum has developed a stock screener which uses a combination of analyst consensus on EBITDA, cash flows, and the most recent balance sheet data. Each quarter, we revisit those assumptions and update the stock price targets accordingly. In a separate blog, I have detailed our analysis methodology.

That EBIT is expected to end up at the lower end of the guided range for FY26 is disappointing. However, we note that analysts are still expecting 4% growth. That is a downward revision from the 9% growth expected earlier, but we note that Jet2 stock is undervalued against is earnings for the prior years. I have set my price target based on the trailing-year figures, which indicates a $23.45 price target. For that reason, I see no good reason to alter my rating for the stock. The company trades at a sharp discount compared to its median EV/EBITDA, and an even steeper discount compared to its peers. We should note that the peer group I have selected is the airline peer group. The company actually is better characterized as a travel and leisure company. However, that peer group has an even higher EV/EBITDA. So, I have opted to benchmark against the peer group with the lower trading multiple.

If you look at the share price movement, you would almost forget that prior year earnings are not even valued in at current and prior price levels. The downward adjustment to EBITDA had a 60 basis point impact on EBITDA margins. However, we do note that operating cash flow remains strong despite 2026 shaping up as a downbeat year. CapEx as a share of operating cash flow is expected to increase as the company is taking delivery of Airbus A321neo airplanes to drive down costs. The company’s cash balance is expected to remain healthy with continued debt reductions while maintaining a net cash position. The current stock price reflects none of that.

Conclusion: Jet2 Valuation Makes No Sense

The company will provide an update later in November on the expected performance this year. Until there is more clarity, there may be more pressure on the share price, as it is possible net analyst estimates for this year and next year will be calibrated. However, with the estimates we currently have, we can safely say that the company is extremely discounted against its own historic valuation levels as well as other airlines, and it almost seems as if the market cannot appreciate the business or the company’s net cash position, which is definitely not something that all airlines or travel names have.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: BrainsWay: High Growth Set To Persist
data.description:
BrainsWay's (BWAY) business continues to thrive, with growth surprising to the upside in Q3 and margins reaching a record high. The company also maintains a strong balance sheet, which is enabling it to make investments in both customers and related technologies. I am not enthralled by the investments in TMS service providers though, as these are structured like a loan and appear to be a method of driving sales.

Ongoing improvements in profitability are somewhat surprising as BrainsWay previously suggested that it was going to increase its investments in R&D. R&D spend has only increased modestly in 2025 though and there has been limited discussion of trials to expand the addressable market.

I previously suggested that the market was ignoring BrainsWay's strong growth prospects, and the stock is up around 60% since then. BrainsWay's growth is likely to remain fairly solid going forward, which should drive its stock higher over time. I am now more neutral on the company though, as its valuation has become quite high and there is uncertainty around market size and the quality of revenue.

Market Conditions

BrainsWay's business is thriving at the moment, which is at least in part due to strong end market demand. While publicly available data on the TMS market is limited, internet search volumes indicates growing general awareness.

BrainsWay's business has also likely been aided by the fact that financing is now easier for customers to obtain. This should be less of an issue for BrainsWay now anyway as it has shifted focus to larger organizations and is concentrating on leases rather than capital sales.

BrainsWay Business Updates

BrainsWay previously stated that it was going to conduct a clinical trial with its Deep TMS 360 system (rotating magnetic field) in the second half of 2025. There had also been mention of feasibility studies in areas like Alzheimer's disease and Parkinson's. R&D appears to have been deemphasized though, with the company's focus shifting to commercialization. This isn't necessarily a bad thing given BrainsWay's recent strong growth, but additional indications will be needed to drive growth long-term.

Development work is still ongoing, with BrainsWay recently receiving clearance for its accelerated Deep TMS protocol for MDD. While efficacy is similar to standard TMS, the accelerated protocol occurs over 6 days rather than 4 weeks of daily treatment. This should open up new opportunities due to the increased convenience. The immediate financial impact of this could be muted by the fact that reimbursement currently only covers 2 treatments per day.

BrainsWay also recently received a NIH grant (2.5 million USD over 5 years) for research into the treatment of alcohol use disorder using TMS. Participants will receive 3 treatments per day over 10 consecutive business days. BrainsWay previously received a multi-million dollar NIH grant covering the study of TMS for methamphetamine use disorder. I am fairly pessimistic on the use of TMS for addiction as it is both expensive and time consuming, which is exemplified by TMS as a smoking cessation aid. BrainsWay received FDA clearance for this several years ago but it now appears to be a forgotten part of the business, which likely relates to a lack of demand and difficulty obtaining reimbursement.

BrainsWay made a 5 million USD investment in Stella MSO earlier in the year. Stella is a management services organization that services more than 20 mental health clinics in Israel and the US. BrainsWay's stake is in the form of a preferred, annually compounding security with a redemption mechanism, which makes the investment closer to a loan than an equity investment. BrainsWay also made a similar investment in Axis Integrated Mental Health. Axis is a management services organization servicing several mental health clinics in Colorado. BrainsWay initially invested 2.3 million USD, with the potential for another 1 million USD milestone-based investment. In addition, the company more recently invested in another 2 US mental health providers. These were for similar amounts of money and with the investments having the same structure. BrainsWay wants to make one more investment this year and several more next year, and has a pipeline of opportunities that it is working through due diligence on.

These relationships are likely important drivers of growth at the moment, as each organization is expected to open 10-15 new clinics each year. BrainsWay also suggested that initial collaborations with customers has led to a more than 50% improvement in system utilization.

BrainsWay has also made a small investment (5 million USD convertible note) in Neurolief, the developer of a neuromodulation device that can be administered outside of a clinic. The agreement also involves milestone-based funding and gives BrainsWay the option of acquiring Neurolief. The ProlivRx device is pending Premarket Approval from the FDA for the treatment of MDD, which is expected toward the end of the year. The company's RelivionMG therapy is already approved in the US, Europe and Japan for the treatment of migraines.

Financial Analysis

BrainsWay generated 13.5 million USD revenue in the third quarter, an increase of 29% YoY. The company shipped 90 systems, up 43% YoY, bringing its total installed base to over 1,600 systems. The fact that system shipments are rapidly outpacing revenue likely stems from BrainsWay's current focus on leasing. Approximately 70% of recent customer engagements have reportedly been lease agreements.

BrainsWay now expects 51-52 million USD revenue in 2025, an increase of 25% over 2024 at the midpoint. Guidance implies roughly 21% YoY growth in the fourth quarter. Even with an abnormally large guidance beat, BrainsWay's growth appears set to fall to its lowest level since the middle of 2023 though. Growth is likely to be sustained in the near-term as BrainsWay's book-to-bill ratio remains well over 1, and its remaining performance obligations total 65 million USD.

BrainsWay's operating profit was roughly 1.3 million USD in the third quarter at a 9% margin. The company's adjusted EBITDA was 2 million USD, while free cash flow was a little less than 600 million USD. Free cash flow has been volatile in recent quarters though, driven by large swings in deferred revenue. Overall, BrainsWay's margins continue to trend higher as the business scales, and this situation should persist going forward.

BrainsWay ended the third with 70.7 million USD cash and cash equivalents. The company also has no debt and only around 6.7 million USD in lease liabilities. This balance sheet strength is likely to be directed towards further minority investments in customers, and potentially related technologies.

Conclusion

BrainsWay's growth remains strong, and its margins are improving as the business scales. This situation appears set to persist going forward, as bookings continue to outpace billings and BrainsWay retains a large quantity of remaining performance obligations.

While this is positive, and should see the stock continue to move higher, some of this growth appears to be related to equity investments that are more like loans in spirit. There may not be anything wrong with this arrangement, particularly in a higher interest rate environment, but it is hard not to feel that this is lower quality revenue.

More importantly, the failure to return capital to investors at a depressed valuation has taken much of the potential upside of the table. BrainsWay could continue to generate strong returns going forward, but I have doubts about the size of the market. The company's installed base was only around 1,350 at the end of 2024 and BrainsWay seems set to place 400-500 systems in 2026. How long this type of growth can be maintained before the market saturates is unknown, making paying a high multiple for the stock risky.

data.title: Reddit's 20% User Growth Proves The ChatGPT Panic Is Misplaced
data.description:
Investment Thesis

Reddit (RDDT) offers investors one of my favorite setups. This is a classic Deep Value Returns situation. There's a lot of uncertainty and worry overhanging a company with terrific fundamentals.

So, if this is good, what's the risk? Glad you asked! Let's address it head-on.

As succinctly as possible: We’re in a moment of uncertainty. No one, myself included, can say with absolute conviction how this will play out. Perhaps LLMs will eat into Reddit’s engagement. Who knows?

But in my experience, even if the bad news does percolate through and there’s a slowdown in revenue growth, what investors truly crave is visibility. Even if it’s bad news. They just want to know where the company's growth will stabilize at.

Presently, Reddit's daily users are up roughly 20% year-over-year. Meanwhile, ChatGPT has been used by the wider public for more than two years. If ChatGPT were killing off Reddit, the business wouldn't be delivering this sort of growth. No way, Jose.

There's no need to overcomplicate matters. I pride myself on following a very simple and easy-to-follow setup. Consequently, I'm very bullish on RDDT.

Reddit's Near-Term Prospects

Reddit is a very well-known platform among investors. You don't really need any introduction.

Its appeal? It is how unfiltered Reddit feels. You don’t need to know anyone’s background—what matters is what you’re interested in. People go there for genuine opinions, not some polished-up marketing spin. Got that so far?

Now, let's get to the drama. Investors right now are torn. Many investors argue that LLMs, like ChatGPT, will replace the user's need to utilize Reddit. And will it? Look, I'll be honest. I don't have a clue. Maybe it will, maybe it won't; who knows? And that's the problem. This uncertainty.

While I believe that Reddit and LLMs can coexist, since they serve different needs—LLMs are great for synthesis, and Reddit is more about human perspective. Reddit has always been messy and emotional, and that’s not something an AI can fully replicate.

But that's neither here nor there. What we have right now is this period of uncertainty. And the one thing that investors hate, second only to bad news, is uncertainty.

And being too focused on the future uncertainty, misses its medium-term potential. Why?

Because focusing on its long-term prospects misses the fact that when we actually know to what degree Reddit can coexist with ChatGPT and other LLMs, the uncertainty will have been removed, and this opportunity will have been re-rated higher by then.

Add to that the fact that they’re finally turning a profit, and the story still makes sense. It’s not without risk; there's never a free lunch anywhere. But the risk-reward is just too compelling for me.

2026 Could Be on a Path to 40% Y/Y

I think 40%-plus revenue growth in 2026 is a realistic target for Reddit.

Sure, Q2 will be a tough compare. Case in point, last year revenue jumped 78%. But if you step back and look at the year as a whole, it looks much more manageable.

Given this context, let's now discuss the opportunity by focusing on its valuation. And yet, we'll discuss its risks too!

RDDT Stock Valuation - 38x Forward Free Cash Flow

Now, on valuation. As an Inflection investor, I always start with the balance sheet. Because I believe that cash is like oxygen. Nobody thinks about it until they need it. And when you start worrying about it, it's already too late. You don’t want to be at the mercy of creditors.

On this front, Reddit’s got about $2.2B in cash and no debt—nearly 6% of its market cap. This is a Fort Knox-like balance sheet.

And yet, keep in mind that advertising is highly seasonal, so Q4 tends to shine strong. Based on that, I see Reddit doing about $620M in free cash flow for 2025. If revenue grows around 40% year-over-year in 2026, free cash flow could climb 60% to roughly $1B—and that’s me being conservative. This is just how operating leverage works.

At that level, Reddit’s trading around 38x next year’s free cash flow. That puts it at an adjusted PEG ratio of about 1x—and generally, anything under 3x is worth a look. Note, I swap in future FCF for earnings in my adjusted PEG ratio.

So yes, there are risks, but the setup still looks solid heading into 2026.

Risk Factors to Consider

My whole Reddit thesis really hangs on one thing: that Reddit keeps growing revenue around 40% y/y, which, I believe, is very possible, since its comparables next year will be so much easier than this year.

What I don’t want to see is that its revenue growth is slowing to 35% y/y or less, particularly if it turns out that Reddit’s already presold too much of its data to ChatGPT and other AI models. If that happens, I’d be on the wrong side of the trade, and I'll step aside.

The risk here is pretty simple: if people start getting answers straight from ChatGPT instead of browsing Reddit threads, traffic drops, and naturally, so will Reddit's price power and ad revenue.

That said, I see this more as a narrative overhang than a real threat. I've seen this movie tons of times before with other names—investors panic, the story gets cloudy, and everyone starts looking to the share price as vindication of the business being in peril. But if free cash flow keeps climbing, the stock will inflect higher.

The Bottom Line

In sum, I see the current investor fear and uncertainty around Reddit as just noise.

Is it frustrating? Absolutely! But that’s what creates opportunity. Once investors gain more visibility into how Reddit’s growth stabilizes and co-exists alongside LLMs, sentiment will shift fast.

It's a bit like people didn't believe that Spotify could work alongside pirated music or free music on YouTube. Sometimes the market is too mercurial.

But here, the fundamentals speak loudly: a rock-solid balance sheet, while Reddit is on a path to $1B of free cash flow in 2026.

Yes, investors are fearful and worried and whatever else. What if? What if?

While for my part, I'm fully focused on its free cash flow. And see this risk-reward as just too compelling not to get involved.

There was minimal trading activity in the portfolio during the third quarter. There were no holdings liquidated and only one new position established: WaterBridge Infrastructure, LLC. WaterBridge is the largest pure-play integrated water infrastructure company in the U.S., with operations predominantly in the Permian Basin, one of the most prolific oil and natural gas basins in the world. The company specializes in handling produced water, which is water that comes out of the ground along with oil or natural gas during exploration and production.

Managing that water (transporting, storing, recycling/disposal) is a big operational and environmental challenge for oil & gas producers. WaterBridge aims to be the most reliable and cost-effective solution for water management to meet the E&P industry’s evolving water handling needs.

At quarter end, the Fund held the common stock of 25 companies, across a broad array of industries, with an emphasis on royalty companies engaged in land, water and natural resource management, basic materials, and financial services. Texas Pacific Land Corporation remains the largest holding in the Fund at 16.6% of assets.

Thank you for being a shareholder in the Ave Maria Value Focused Fund.

IMPORTANT INFORMATION FOR INVESTORS

As of 9-30-25, the holding percentages of the stocks mentioned in this commentary are as follows: Pan American Silver Corporation (2.7%), Wheaton Precious Metals Corporation (5.3%), Franco-Nevada Corporation (6.0%), Permian Basin Royalty Trust (3.2%), Genuine Parts Company (4.4%), The Simply Good Foods Company (1.5%), Chemed Corporation (1.9%), Haemonetics Corporation (1.1%), LandBridge Company, LLC (6.4%), Texas Pacific Land Corporation (16.6%) and WaterBridge Infrastructure, LLC. (4.4%). Fund holdings are subject to change and should not be considered purchase recommendations. Smith Corporation (4.8%), Chevron Corporation (4.8%), WaterBridge Infrastructure LLC (4.4%), Genuine Parts Company (4.4%), ConocoPhillips (4.2%) and XPEL, Inc. (4.2%). The most current available data regarding portfolio holdings can be found on our website, www.avemariafunds.com. Current and future portfolio holdings are subject to risk.

Because the fund may invest in securities of smaller numbers of issuers, the Fund may be more exposed to the risks associated with and developments affecting an individual issuer than a fund that invests more widely. This may, therefore, have a greater impact on the Fund’s performance. Performance can be affected by market and investment style risk. Mutual Fund investing involves risk and principal loss is possible. The Fund’s investments in small- and mid-capitalization companies could experience greater volatility than investments in large-capitalization companies. Prior to April 28, 2025, the Ave Maria Value Focused Fund was named the Schwartz Value Focused Fund.

The investment performance assumes reinvestment of dividends and capital gains distributions. The S&P 1500® Index includes all stocks in the S&P 500®, S&P 400®, and S&P 600®. This index covers 90% of the market capitalization of U.S. stocks. The S&P 500® Index is a capitalization weighted unmanaged index of 500 widely traded stocks, created by Standard & Poor’s. The index is considered to represent the performance of the stock market in general. Indexes do not incur fees and it is not possible to invest directly in an index.

Morningstar Percentile Rankings is the fund’s total-return percentile rank relative to all funds that have the same Morningstar Category. The highest (or most favorable) percentile rank is 1 and the lowest (or least favorable) percentile rank is 100. The top-performing fund in a category will always receive a rank of 1. In the Morningstar Mid-Cap Blend Fund Category, the Fund had the following percentile rankings: 1 year (5th out of 419 funds), 3 years (7th out of 373 funds ), 5 years (1st out of 348 funds) and 10 years (2nd out of 254 funds). Morningstar Rating™ for funds, or “star rating,” is calculated for managed products (including mutual funds, variable annuity and variable life subaccounts, exchange-traded funds, closed-end funds, and separate accounts) with at least a three-year history. Exchange-traded funds and open-ended mutual funds are considered a single population for comparative purposes. It is calculated based on a Morningstar Risk-Adjusted Return measure that accounts for variation in a managed product’s monthly excess performance, placing more emphasis on downward variations and rewarding consistent performance. The top 10% of products in each product category receive 5 stars, the next 22.5% receive 4 stars, the next 35% receive 3 stars, the next 22.5% receive 2 stars, and the bottom 10% receive 1 star. The Overall Morningstar Rating for a managed product is derived from a weighted average of the performance figures associated with its three-, five-, and 10-year (if applicable) Morningstar Rating metrics. The weights are: 100% three-year rating for 36-59 months of total returns, 60% five-year rating/40% three-year rating for 60-119 months of total returns, and 50% 10-year rating/30% five-year rating/20% three- year rating for 120 or more months of total returns. While the 10-year overall star rating formula seems to give the most weight to the 10-year period, the most recent three-year period actually has the greatest impact because it is included in all three rating periods. A 4- or 5-star rating does not necessarily imply that a fund has achieved positive results for the period. data.title: Perion Network: Solid Quarter, Constructive Outlook - Buy
data.description:
Note:

I have covered Perion Network Ltd. or "Perion" (NASDAQ:PERI), previously, so investors should view this as an update to my earlier articles on the company.

Earlier this week, Perion Network reported better-than-expected third quarter results, with year-over-year growth in both display and search advertising:

Following a disappointing Connected TV ("CTV") performance last quarter, the channel rebounded with an impressive 75% year-over-year sales growth:

While web advertising sales were down both on a quarter-over-quarter and year-over-year basis, this was mostly a result of the company's recent decision to exit certain lower margin activities, as outlined by management on the conference call.

Operating cash flow of $5.9 million was impacted by net unfavorable working capital movements. However, management guided for a full-year cash flow conversion rate of 70% which should translate into decent cash generation in Q4.

Perion finished the quarter with $315.6 million in cash and liquid investments, down slightly on a sequential basis due to ongoing share repurchases, as discussed in more detail by management on the conference call:

During the quarter, we continued to execute our share buyback program. Due to regulatory volume restrictions, our repurchase activity was limited, leading to the repurchase of 800,000 shares for a total amount of $7.5 million.

As of September 30, 2025, we have repurchased a cumulative total of 10.4 million shares for $94.2 million.

This continued buyback commitment underscored our confidence in Perion's long-term value proposition.

As of today, during the fourth quarter, we already purchased an additional 1 million shares, and we expect to complete the current plan by the end of this year.

The board of directors also approved a $75 million expansion of the company's share repurchase program:

Looking ahead, Perion reaffirmed previously provided full-year expectations:

For 2026, management expects to scale the company's recently established Perion One platform:

We are expanding Perion One across more channels, deepening adoption with global brands and increasing recurring high-margin revenue streams, powered by AI, automation and self-service capabilities.

Each step brings us closer to our vision: a single intelligent platform that delivers better performance, faster decisions and long-term value for marketers and shareholders.

During the questions-and-answers session, some analysts appeared to be disappointed by management's decision to stick with full-year guidance despite another quarter of operational outperformance.

One analyst outright asked if current consensus growth expectations for 2026 might be too high, but the CFO pointed to quite the opposite:

(...) we actually believe that we will be able to beat next year as well the Street expectation. And our growth engines will continue to outperform the market, and I believe that it will drive our growth going forward.

And we are -- as much as we are focusing on the efficiency and we start to see this in terms of the numbers starting already for this Q, we are also investing in our current business to allow us to scale and to grow much faster also for next year.

Should the company manage to close 2025 on a strong note and provide initial guidance ahead of rather muted expectations, I would expect shares to reach new recovery highs following last year's search advertising disaster.

My unchanged price target of $14.75 remains based on a targeted 2026 EV/Adjusted EBITDA multiple of 5x, which represents a more than 60% discount to the U.S. advertising industry.

However, it will take more time and strong execution for the valuation gap to narrow after recent missteps.

As a result, I am reiterating my Buy rating on Perion Network's common shares.

Risk Factors

As usual, the bull thesis is not without risks. Should the company underperform growth expectations in the final quarter of the year and/or provide underwhelming 2026 guidance, the stock would likely face renewed selling pressure.

Bottom Line

Perion Network reported better-than-expected third quarter results, driven by an impressive rebound in CTV revenues.

The company continues to buy back shares in the open market and recently expanded its share repurchase authorization by $75 million.

While Perion only reaffirmed full-year guidance, management projected 2026 growth to exceed current consensus expectations.

Following last year's search advertising disaster, the company continues to trade at a steep discount to the broader industry. However, it will require more time and continued execution for the valuation gap to narrow.

At this point, I am reiterating my Buy rating on Perion Network's shares, with an unchanged price target of $14.75.

data.title: Meta: Why The Recent Selloff Is A Buying Opportunity (Rating Upgrade)
data.description:
Is Higher Spending Always a Good Thing?

Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) took a big hit after releasing Q3 FY2025 results. The selloff wiped out 50% of the entire rally since April 4th. Is the market overreacting? First of all, META recorded a $16 billion one-time expense in Q3 due to the President Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill Act" (which created a $6.2 diluted EPS headwind). This caused a big EPS miss, but it was not the reason for the selloff. Many companies recorded similar expenses in their recent quarters.

I believe the real reason is about META's aggressive tone on its AI spending outlook. In my last analysis, I maintained a Buy rating, because previous AI investment cycles have clearly drive strong growth for the company. The expanded AI capabilities have integrated with its family of apps, driving persistent +20% growth in advertising revenue. I also noted that this aggressive AI spending will continue this year, with capex as a percentage of total revenue expected to rise to 36% in FY2025, up from 23% in FY2024.

Now, we are seeing even more aggressive capex in FY2026, which is concerning. First, it will pressure margins, earnings, and FCF in the near-term, lowering the stock's quality. Second, these capex takes time to monetize and largely depend on management's execution, especially as the market worries about potential economic slowdown.

After a 19% post-earnings selloff, the stock's valuation has reset. I know that META's long-term GenAI roadmap requires higher upfront investments, deserving some risk premium (which means a higher discount rate and lower valuation), but I would also argue that the stock is already trading at a low multiple, with 21.4x non-GAAP P/E FY2026, assuming a flat YoY EPS growth. Therefore, the dip is worth buying, so I'm upgrading META to Strong Buy.

Q3 FY2025: Don't Focus Only on Revenue Growth

META's total revenue beat the high end of guidance every quarter this year including 3Q. Advertising revenue was up 25.6% YoY, showing continued acceleration from 21.5% YoY in the previous quarter. Without the one-time, non-cash charge, META should have topped EPS by a wide margin.

However, investors are not focused on META's revenue. The stock sold off because they are worried about the bottom-line, which I will discuss in the next section.

For Q4, META guided revenue to a range of $56 to $59 billion (including a 1% currency tailwind), which appears little light. The midpoint implies 19.9% YoY growth, as indicated on the chart above. Based on the recent quarters, my base case is that 4Q revenue could come in near $59 billion (implying a 22% YoY growth).

During the Q3 FY2025 earnings call, management said average price per ad jumped 10% YoY, driven by stronger advertiser demand and improved monetization. They also saw demand pulled forward for Quest headsets ahead of the holiday season.

Q3 Margin Contraction Driven by Higher R&D Costs

For earnings, Q3 diluted EPS would have been $7.25 per share excluding the tax charge. This suggests a 20.1% YoY growth. So why did the bottom line slow when revenue accelerated? That could be one of the key reasons why investors dumped META's shares. The company operating margin contracted by 200 bps on a sequential basis.

Looking at the table, R&D as a percentage of total revenue reached a record high, up by 300 bps QoQ while other expense segments remained stable. During the earnings call, the CFO said that compensation costs jumped significantly due to hiring more AI talent and higher infrastructure-related operating costs. This is what investors are nervous about.

FY2026 Will Be a Year of "Inefficiency"

META has been very committed to AI investments over the past few years, and management continues to raise the FY2025 capex outlook every quarter. In Q3, they raised the low end of the full-year capex range by $4 billion, now expecting $70-72 billion.

Q3 capex continued accelerating to 110.5% YoY, up from 100.8% YoY in Q2. Capex as a percentage of total revenue reached a new high of 37.8%. If we consider a $71 billions capex outlook (the midpoint), this suggests a 41% YoY growth in 4Q FY2025. This number still looks strong, especially when it compares to an 87.8% YoY in Q4 FY2024.

Even if FY2025 capex growth is expected to increase 81% YoY, management continues to pump even more money into AI investment in FY2026. In the earnings call, they guided that "capex dollar growth will be notably larger in 2026 than 2025" and "total expenses will grow at a significantly faster percentage rate in 2026."

I believe the market is expecting a big drop in margins in FY2026, and total revenue growth will likely normalize compared to this year (slowing on YoY basis). This could cause a sharp slowdown in earnings. All of these shows a deterioration in fundamentals. However, META needs to expand its AI infrastructure capacity to win over the long-term, but investors are worried about overspending in the wrong macro environment, which is a risk we can't ignore.

FCF Profile Is Ugly and Going to be Uglier

The strong capex will cause a big drop in FCF this year. So far, we have seen a continued decline in FCF over the past three quarters, and the YoY decline is getting bigger. FCF was down 31.5% YoY in Q3. Given more aggressive capex outlook in FY2026, we will likely see further FCF declines, which lower the stock's quality. However, after this major pullback, its valuation has already priced in a higher risk premium.

No Valuation Bubble

The GAAP P/E trades at 27.7x, nearly unchanged from 28x since my last rating four months ago. This means the stock's multiple hasn't expanded at all. On a non-GAAP basis, the P/E FY2025 is around 21.4x, adjusting for Q3 tax charge. Assuming a flat EPS over the next 12 months, trading at a 21.4x non-GAAP P/E FY2026 looks very attractive to me.

META is now trading at a lower P/E than its 5-year average of 22.9x. It's also significantly lower than GOOGL's 27.7x. META is the cheapest stock among the "Magnificent 7," which is why I believe the recent selloff is a strong buying opportunity.

Conclusion

In summary, META's recent selloff is overdone. It's fair for investors to worry about higher capex in FY2026, since larger cost structure will pressure margins, earnings, and FCF, which can lower the stock's near-term quality. But, META's long-term goal is beyond the advertising market. If META wants to maintain its current growth momentum and build top-tier GenAI leadership, it has to keep spending on AI. Of course, some investors may still be skeptical about the risk of overspending, but META's ROI from previous cycles has been strong. Given trading at 21x forward P/E now, I believe the risk and reward profile is very attractive.

data.title: Oakmark International Small Cap Strategy Q3 2025 Commentary
data.description:
Performance highlights

Contributors

  • Wynn Macau (OTCPK:WYNMF)
  • Azimut Holding (OTCPK:AZIHF)
  • JDE Peet's (OTCPK:JDEPF)

Detractors

  • Amplifon (OTCPK:AMFPF)
  • LANXESS (OTCPK:LNXSF)
  • Hays (OTCPK:HAYPF)

Market Environment

International equities finished higher during the quarter, with 10 of 11 GICS sectors posting positive returns. By sector, financial and industrials contributed the most to market returns while consumer staples was the sole detractor. By country, Japan and Canada contributed the most to market performance, while Denmark and Germany detracted.

Portfolio Performance

The portfolio’s return was 0.97% (net) for the reporting period. This compares to the MSCI World ex USA Small Cap Index that returned 7.24% for the same period.

Top contributors:

Wynn Macau was a contributor during the quarter. The Hong Kong-based casino operator saw its stock price rise despite delivering underwhelming second-quarter 2025 results. This performance was supported by a strengthening Macau market in recent months, which has benefited Wynn as one of just six licensed operators in the city—and among the most efficient. We are encouraged by the company’s near-term plans to introduce updated rooms and a revamped Chairman’s Club, which should bolster its position in the premium segment. Wynn remains well-positioned for continued outperformance.

Azimut Holdings was a contributor during the quarter. The Italian financials firm’s stock price rose as it posted solid results during the period, in our view. We continue to appreciate management's focus on long-term value creation. We think the company remains strongly positioned, as it could be a beneficiary of strong macroeconomic tailwinds.

JDE Peet’s was a contributor during the quarter. The Netherlands-headquartered coffee company’s stock price steamed ahead after announcing that it would be acquired by American beverage giant Keurig Dr Pepper in January 2026. Keurig Dr Pepper agreed to purchase JDE Peet’s for EUR 31.85 per share plus an interim dividend, shy of our estimated fair value but a solid premium over its share price at the time. We believe there is a high probability that the deal will go through, as the largest shareholder will vote in favor of the transaction, and regulatory concerns are minimal. We have pared back our JDE Peet’s position in recognition of its recent gains. Still, we remain invested in the company due to its strong market position in what we view as an attractive consumer packaged goods segment.

Top detractors:

Amplifon was a detractor during the quarter. The Italian hearing aid company’s stock price plunged after first half results revealed that revenue grew more slowly than expected, and the company reduced its fiscal year guidance. While this news was disappointing, we believe this shortfall is due to market and cyclical issues in the U.S. and several European markets rather than a structural slowing of hearing aid demand. We appreciate the front-footed management team which launched a new cost program to offset the impact from the revenue shortfall. We also believe that Amplifon’s strong market position and ability to earn attractive profits remains intact. As such, Amplifon remains an attractive holding in our view.

Lanxess was a detractor during the quarter. The German-headquartered global specialty chemicals company’s stock price declined after it delivered results. The company decreased its full-year profit guidance, citing weakened demand and a challenging environment. Still, we see Lanxess as a well-managed company with a long runway for future growth.

Hays was a detractor during the quarter. The U.K.-headquartered human resources and recruitment firm’s stock price declined due to broader market weakness and uncertainty. Still, the company remains well-positioned in markets that we find attractive. Over time, we think management can help the company bolster its profitability as conditions become more favorable.

Portfolio Positioning

We initiated the following position(s) during the period:

Alten (OTCPK:ABLGF) is one of the world’s largest engineering service providers, partnering with companies to help them design, execute, and manage project-based engineering and IT services. Over time, R&D intensity is rising as a percentage of GDP as corporations invest in new technologies and capabilities to differentiate their products, accelerate the speed of product development, and comply with increasing regulation. To help manage this cost, corporations are increasingly outsourcing their R&D spending, making outsourced R&D services a GDP+ growth business through-cycle. Alten is one of a handful of companies in this space that is considered a global tier-1 service provider, which helps them tightly embed with customers in very sticky relationships. The business itself is asset light and very cash generative and is still run by its founder, Simon Azoulay, who we consider a shrewd capital allocator into bolt-on M&A that enhances the company's capabilities, customer list, and/or geographic reach. Alten’s stock price has contracted recently as the engineering services sector undergoes a cyclical downturn made worse by tariff uncertainty. We expect business fundamentals to eventually recover, driving an improvement in growth, profitability, and valuation, which is at historic lows.

Hamamatsu Photonics (OTCPK:HPHTF) is Japan's premier photon specialist, supplying detectors and light sources that enable complex machines to see what the human eye cannot and detect nanometer-scale defects. Hamamatsu is widely regarded as the preferred industry supplier and commands leading market share position across its major product categories. Moreover, the company's partnerships with its customers often begins in the concept stage of development, which has led to sticky, long-term relationships. In addition, we appreciate management's commitment to reinforcing its competitive moat through consistent reinvestment in research development, and believe it is poised to see an inflection point in its share price as cyclical headwinds subside. These headwinds provided us the opportunity to initiate a position in a dominant company with improving shareholder returns that is trading at a significant discount to our estimate of intrinsic value.

JEOL (OTC:JEOLY) manufactures, develops, and sells a range of scientific and industrial equipment. JEOL’s roots lie in its scientific instruments business, which sells tools like electron microscopes to academic institutions and food, chemical, and semiconductor customers, among others. Building and servicing this equipment requires significant scientific know-how, resulting in high barriers to entry and thus oligopolistic market structures. JEOL subsequently leveraged its electron beam technology to manufacture mask writing equipment, which fulfills an integral step in chip manufacturing that tethers the company to the world’s leading-edge foundries. JEOL services mask writers for years after selling them, creating a recurring revenue stream that helps smooth out the volatility of equipment sales. Additionally, management has intensified its focus on improving profitability in the scientific instruments business, where margins meaningfully lag global peers. In sum, we believe we are buying two good franchises – scientific instruments and mask writers – for the price of one.

Kansai Paint (OTCPK:KSANF) is one of the two market leaders in paint and coatings in Japan. The company’s products are used primarily for automobiles, construction, and ships along with bridges and residential housing. We have owned Kansai on and off over the past 15 years. We most recently sold out of the company in early 2024 as the share price reached our estimate of intrinsic value. Since then, the share price has declined more than 20% due to weak auto production and a weak decorative market in India as well as a de-rating of most global paint companies. Since then, management has focused on shifting emphasis to niches within the paint market, such as construction chemicals and wood coating while focusing less on retail. With the shares priced at more attractive levels, we reinitiated a position in the company. After speaking with management, we continue to believe that there is ample opportunity to reduce costs and improve profitability. Additionally, we believe many of the negatives of the Indian decorative market are now priced into the shares. Finally, we think management continues to allocate capital well and plans to return 100% of free cash flow through dividends and share repurchases.

Robertet (OTCPK:RBTEF) is a global leader in the production of flavors and fragrances and a near pure-play on the natural ingredients sub-segment. The company supplies premium components to high-end luxury brands such as Hermès, Chanel, and Aesop. Although these inputs represent a small portion of end products' cost of goods sold, they are critical to the consumer experience, defining the signature scent or taste and enabling Robertet to command premium pricing. Many of these scents and flavors underpin popular heritage products, leading to long-lived, recurring revenues. Despite its strong positioning, the stock trades at a discount to our estimate of intrinsic value due to cyclical headwinds and lingering investor overhang, which we believe are temporary. We appreciate Robertet's shareholder-aligned management team and believe their renewed efforts to expand, internationalize, and modernize the business will lead to significant value creation over the long term.

Aumovio (OTC:AMVOY) spun out of Continental in September 2025 and now develops, manufactures and distributes auto components and technology systems as a standalone company. Even without the backing of Continental’s tires business, there’s a lot to like about Aumovio. It built up strong positions in structurally attractive markets over the years under Continental. Aumovio operates through several segments that expose it to a compelling range of auto megatrends, like active safety, autonomous driving and software. Those trends have long teased upside, and we believe recent restructuring, portfolio optimization and a focus on returns will help Aumovio realize its potential. CEO Philipp von Hirschheydt and his management team deserve credit for that improved execution, and now that he’s armed with a cash-rich balance sheet following the spinoff, we expect his knack for underpromising and overdelivering to continue. We were excited to open a position in Aumovio at a price we believe is well below its intrinsic value.

We eliminated the following position(s) during the period:

  • BIPROGY
  • Hakuhodo DY Holdings (OTCPK:HKUOY)
  • Sapiens International (US Shs) (SPNS)

Outlook

For more than a decade, those of us who practice “value investing”—especially in overseas markets—witnessed U.S. growth/momentum stocks soar. We asserted that this trend was unsustainable as it was mostly driven by both weak foreign currencies as well as valuation expansion by U.S. growth stocks. Year-to-date, we are witnessing the unraveling of this paradigm. At the same time, the fundamentals of what we view as the most attractively priced global region, European equities, have improved thus driving more investor interest. As the valuation spread expanded during the last 10 years, our view was that the greater the spread was, the harder it would be to maintain it. We remain confident in our belief that given the large valuation spread that still exists, we will continue to benefit from this change.

Past performance is no guarantee of future results. Current performance may be lower or higher than the performance data quoted. The gross performance presented above does not reflect the deduction of investment advisory fees. All returns reflect the reinvestment of dividends and capital gains and the deduction of transaction costs. The client’s return will be reduced by the advisory fees and other expenses it may incur in the management of its account. The advisory fee, compounded over a period of years, will have an adverse effect on the value of the client’s portfolio.

Understanding the risks

All investments carry a certain degree of risk, including possible loss of principal. There is no assurance that an investment will provide positive performance over any time period. Foreign securities present risks that in some ways may be greater than investments in U.S. investments. Those risks include: currency fluctuation; different regulation, accounting standards, trading practices and levels of available information; generally higher transaction costs; and political risks. Value stocks may fall out of favor with investors and underperform growth stocks during given periods. Smaller companies' stocks often involve more risk than the stocks of larger companies. Stocks of small companies tend to be more volatile and have a smaller public market than stocks of larger companies. Small companies may have a shorter history of operations than larger companies, may not have as great an ability to raise additional capital and may have a less diversified product line, making them more susceptible to market pressure.

This material is not intended to be a recommendation or investment advice, does not constitute a solicitation to buy, sell or hold a security or an investment strategy, and is not provided in a fiduciary capacity. The information provided does not take into account the specific objectives or circumstances of any particular investor or suggest any specific course of action. Investment decisions should be made based on an investor’s objectives and circumstances and in consultation with his or her advisors.

The information, data, analyses, and opinions presented herein (including current investment themes, the portfolio managers’ research and investment process, and portfolio characteristics) are for informational purposes only and represent the investments and views of the portfolio managers and Harris Associates L.P. as of the date written and are subject to change without notice.

The specific securities identified and described in this report do not represent all the securities purchased, sold, or recommended to advisory clients. There is no assurance that any securities discussed herein will remain in an account’s portfolio at the time one receives this report or that securities sold have not been repurchased. It should not be assumed that any of the securities, transactions, or holdings discussed herein were or will prove to be profitable.

Glossary

The MSCI World ex USA Small Cap Index (Net) is designed to measure performance of small-cap stocks across 22 of 23 Developed Markets (excluding the United States). The index covers approximately 14% of the free float-adjusted market capitalization in each country. This benchmark calculates reinvested dividends net of withholding taxes. This index is unmanaged and investors cannot invest directly in this index.

The MSCI World ex USA Small Cap Value Index (Net) captures small cap securities exhibiting overall value style characteristics across 22 of 23 Developed Markets countries (excluding the United States). The value investment style characteristics for index construction are defined using three variables: book value-to-price, 12-month forward earnings-to-price, and dividend yield. The Total Return Index (Net) includes reinvested dividends net of foreign withholding tax. This index is unmanaged and investors cannot invest directly in this index.

data.title: Considering The Fair Value Of Markets Amid Valuation Jitters
data.description:
With concerns about valuations, are there any pockets of the market that are still undervalued? David Sekera, Chief U.S. Market Strategist with Morningstar Research joins MoneyTalk to discuss how the fair value of markets has changed.

Transcript

Greg Bonnell: A rising tide in the markets have been lifting stocks higher this year but with valuation concerns around some of those high-flying AI names, putting some jitters into the markets. Are there still any sectors that look undervalued? Joining us now to discuss, David Sekera, Chief US Market Strategist with Morningstar Research.

David, great to have you back on the program.

David Sekera: Hey, Greg, good to see you again.

Greg Bonnell: Let's start with those valuations, seems to be the big concern for investors right now trying to wrap their heads around it. Morningstar, I know they do fair value. We talk about it when you're on the program. It's changing their view on some of the big tech names.

David Sekera: Well, I too am trying to wrap my head around some of these numbers as well. Just the numbers we're talking about are just astronomical. So, for example, in just October, we increased our fair value on 170 different stocks under our coverage. Our increase has well outpaced our decreases by over a 3-to-1 ratio. And in fact, those valuation increases on the 6 largest stocks that we increased our fair values was $3.1 trillion worth of market cap.

Just to put that in perspective, think about it-- that's equal to adding 31 $100 billion companies to the total market valuation. And in the US, there's only 140 companies in the US Market Index with a market cap of over $100 billion. So our biggest increase was on Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL). In fact, we increased our fair value twice on Google over the course of the month. First was after they announced their partnership with Anthropic, and then second, after earnings came in better than what we expected. The total increase in market cap on Alphabet was $1.2 trillion. Now, to put that in perspective, that's larger than the entire market cap of Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A, BRK.B), which in and of itself is the 10th largest in the index.

We also increased our fair value on NVIDIA (NVDA) by $800 billion. That was after the CEO disclosed that they had over $500 billion worth of cumulative sales here in the end of calendar year 2025 and into 2026. That increase there, that's like adding the entire market capitalization of Walmart to the market index.

So, over the course of October, we had a total market increase of our valuations by $4.2 trillion. And, to some degree, it doesn't even look like it's over yet. We actually just increased our fair value on AMD yesterday following their investor meeting, as we incorporated much higher revenue guidance into our financial model.

Greg Bonnell: When you start to recalculate these valuations, what you consider to be fair value, is this catching up to the reality of the stock's moves already? Or do we still think there could be some more room to run in the space?

David Sekera: Well, there's a couple of different things going on. So, as far as whether or not there's still more room to run, that's going to be very idiosyncratic on each of the individual stocks. Now, the market perspective overall, I don't know-- it still feels like momentum wants to keep us moving higher. The market, according to a composite of our fair values, is still a couple percent undervalued. And I think Wall Street just wants to close out a very strong year with some very strong gains.

Longer term, I think investors really need to prepare themselves that from here, I think returns will be much closer to historically normalized types of returns-- essentially, a blended cost of equity over time. I think in 2026, be ready for bouts of volatility similar to what we saw at the beginning of 2025, just considering how much of a growth story all of these stocks are, considering that we are at pretty high valuations based on our growth expectations. Yeah, any type of negative headlines that could cause any doubt in the AI growth story could certainly put a lot of pressure on stocks in the short term.

But like I said, from an individual stock perspective, when we're looking at these big megacaps, those that we think are most closely tied to not only the AI buildout boom but also have very strong underlying fundamental businesses still look attractive, such as Microsoft (MSFT) and Alphabet. Some of the other ones where they're tied to AI, but the valuations in our mind already incorporate our growth or our base case for growth. NVIDIA, Amazon (AMZN), Broadcom (AVGO) are all still pretty fairly valued, whereas a stock like Apple (AAPL), we still think it's overvalued when we look at the growth trajectory, the company not necessarily really having a significant AI product out there yet. That's one that we think has probably shot too much to the upside.

Greg Bonnell: When you talk about potential for volatility, I've been having conversations saying that the market concentration, which we've been concerned about for a while, which obviously this is an issue that's not going away-- it's probably only getting more concentrated-- could mean even more volatile times ahead. What is happening out there with market concentration?

David Sekera: Exactly. Just with all of these fair value increases and the market rallying on these stocks, the big just keep getting bigger, and the concentration just continues to keep getting more and more concentrated. So, with those mega-cap stocks moving higher, larger percentage of the market-- if you look at the top 10 stocks in the Morningstar US Market Index, they're almost 40% of the total market capitalization. And when you look at those 10 stocks, 8 of them are all tied to AI.

And just to put some of this into perspective, if you look at NVIDIA back in 2020, that was only about 1% of the market capitalization. Now, that's up to 7% in and of itself. So, I just have to remind investors, even if you think you're pretty broadly diversified, you have a substantial amount of exposure in the indices-- which are very broad market indices-- you're still heavily exposed to AI-driven returns. It's been great for the upside the past couple of years. But as you noted, I do think it increases some of that downside risk if we have a sell-off in AI.

So, if you're an investor, and you're looking to reduce that concentration risk, or maybe you just have the lack of appetite for investing in AI at this point, yeah, I would recommend looking into diversifying into maybe the value space. Also look at small-cap stocks. Both of those are areas where we see a lot of attractive opportunities which are trading at discounts to our fair values. But they also have minimal exposure to artificial intelligence.

Greg Bonnell: So yeah, it's interesting, obviously, to take a look at other parts of the market that aren't getting a lot of attention right now. When it comes to the AI trade, I believe you're saying that next year we could see a pivot into people who have been building right now the AI infrastructure. But we're waiting for people to show us how they can utilize it.

David Sekera: Exactly. And I think we'd even talked about this a couple of months ago. I thought that that focus would have already shifted by now. But this year, it's still been all about the AI arms race, all about the buildout boom-- specifically just looking at the amount of growth that we've seen in hardware, data centers, and utilities.

But at some point, someone's got to be able to pay for all of this CapEx. And in order to do that, you need to have new economic value created by artificial intelligence. You need to have companies that will improve their existing products and services, companies that can develop new use cases and drive efficiency gains in order to be able to pay for these-- not even tens of billions, but hundreds of billions of dollars worth of CapEx being spent over the next couple of years.

So, I think next year, we're going to see much more focus identifying those companies that will benefit most from AI. Some of the individual areas that we're scouring and looking for these opportunities are in the software and services. I think that's the easiest area to see where AI can make those improvements. Seeing opportunities in health care and life sciences, research and development. I think the financial services industry will be able to use artificial intelligence to make some very large efficiency gains. E-commerce and social media already using AI. And then other areas, like transportation and logistics, I think, will be long-term beneficiaries.

Original Post
